rationale: The cited paper is used in Experiment 3 when the authors classify candidate items as hedonic versus utilitarian ("e.g., Dhar and Wertenbroch 2000") before selecting stimuli. This is more than a passing background mention because it directly supports how key product categories are operationalized in the citing study. At the same time, the citing paper does not build on the cited paper's core acquisition-versus-forfeiture theory, so the role is mainly a borrowed conceptual/measurement template.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Dhar and Wertenbroch's hedonic-versus-utilitarian framework to pretest and categorize products used as dependent-measure stimuli in Experiment 3. The citation functions as support for construct operationalization rather than as a target of theoretical extension.
